target_phenotype: Morphological cell phenotype — cell and nuclear shape, texture, and intensity features across 4 imaging channels (DNA, microtubules, DNA damage, actin)

readout.measurement: High-content fluorescence imaging of fixed cells
readout.instrument_or_platform: Optical pooled screening (in situ sequencing + imaging)
readout.primary_metric: Morphological feature vectors — genes are clustered by similarity of phenotypic profiles across imaging channels

clustering.method: Leiden clustering on PHATE-embedded phenotypic profiles

notes: Interphase-gated cells only. 4-channel morphology screen capturing DNA, cytoskeleton, DNA damage, and actin. Clusters represent sets of genes whose knockout produces similar morphological phenotypes.
